what's Shallow?
the layperson word. "silver ring." Anyone knows this.
what's Mid?
the seller-competent word. "sterling cuff." Someone selling or shopping this category recognizes it as language of the game.
what's Deep?
the specialist word. "hand-hammered anticlastic cuff." Uttered by makers to each other. She holds it, cluster-tags it, doesn't pretend to appraise.
what's New in box?
factory-sealed, unopened. Priced near or below MSRP; buyer is looking for a discount, not a rare item.
what's Used / pre-owned?
opened, used, condition variable. Priced at a large discount to new; the discount is the point.
what's Refurbished?
used, then restored and certified. Priced between used and new, with a warranty attached; the warranty is the point.
what's laptop / notebook?
portable computer.
what's desktop / tower?
non-portable computer.
what's tablet?
flat touchscreen computer.
what's phone / smartphone?
pocket-sized computer.
what's charger / adapter?
the power-supply cable + brick.
what's battery?
the internal power storage.
what's screen / display / monitor?
the visual output surface.
what's keyboard / mouse / trackpad?
the input devices.
what's camera?
the imaging device (integrated or peripheral).
what's speaker / headphones?
the audio output devices.
what's memory / RAM?
the working memory, measured in GB.
what's storage / hard drive / SSD?
the persistent memory.
what's processor / CPU?
the computing chip.
what's GPU / graphics card?
the graphics chip.
what's operating system / OS?
Windows, macOS, iPadOS, Linux.
what's generation / gen?
the model year of the product line (e.g., "M3 MacBook Air", "13th gen Intel Core").
what's model number?
the specific SKU (e.g., "MacBook Air A2681", "Lenovo ThinkPad T14 Gen 3").
what's UPC?
the universal product code, printed on the box.
what's IMEI / MEID / SN?
device serial identifiers (used to verify origin, warranty, unlock status).
what's factory reset / hard reset?
device returned to original software state, user data wiped.
what's iCloud lock / activation lock / MDM?
enterprise or personal locks that prevent resale until removed.
what's jailbroken / rooted?
modified past factory restrictions; usually a negative for resale.
what's carrier unlocked / factory unlocked?
phone works on any carrier network. Unlocked commands a premium.
what's AppleCare / warranty status?
remaining time on the manufacturer warranty.
what's screen protector / case included?
accessories bundled.
what's OEM / OEM part?
original-equipment-manufacturer, meaning made by the brand. Contrast with aftermarket.
what's aftermarket?
third-party part; usually cheaper, lower perceived quality.
what's authorized dealer?
brand-endorsed reseller; carries warranty transfer.
what's first-party refurbished / manufacturer refurbished?
refurbished by the brand itself. Highest tier of refurbished.
what's third-party refurbished?
refurbished by an independent shop.
what's grade A / B / C?
refurbisher condition grades. A: like new; B: minor cosmetic wear; C: heavy cosmetic wear but functional.
what's Retina / OLED / mini-LED / micro-LED?
display technologies. Each has a resolution and contrast profile buyers search for.
what's 1080p / 4K / 8K?
display resolutions.
what's refresh rate / Hz?
display speed (60Hz standard, 120Hz+ premium).
what's HDR / Dolby Vision?
high dynamic range standards.
what's RAM speed / DDR4 / DDR5 / LPDDR5?
memory standards.
what's PCIe / NVMe?
storage bus / protocol standards.
what's USB-C / Thunderbolt 4 / USB4?
connector + protocol standards.
what's HDMI 2.1 / DisplayPort 2.0?
video output standards.
what's Wi-Fi 6 / Wi-Fi 6E / Wi-Fi 7?
wireless network standards.
what's Bluetooth 5.3?
wireless peripheral standard.
what's cellular / eSIM?
mobile network hardware.
what's water resistance IPXX?
the sealed-against-water rating.
what's battery health / cycle count?
used-battery metrics (a MacBook with 300 cycles is much better than one with 1000).
what's cosmetic condition?
the outside; distinct from functional condition.
what's fully functional?
the seller's assertion that everything works.
what's binning?
how manufacturers grade chips within a run. A binned CPU is a chip that met higher spec inside the same die.
what's base / boost / turbo clock?
CPU clock speeds. Base is guaranteed; boost is opportunistic.
what's P-cores / E-cores?
performance vs efficiency cores in modern hybrid CPUs.
what's unified memory?
Apple Silicon's shared memory architecture between CPU and GPU.
what's thermal throttling?
the CPU/GPU slowing to prevent overheating.
what's VRAM?
video memory; separate from system RAM in discrete GPUs.
what's integrated graphics / iGPU?
GPU sharing the CPU die.
what's discrete graphics / dGPU?
separate graphics card.
what's TDP?
thermal design power; the sustained wattage the cooling system is designed for.
what's AVX / AVX-512 / AMX?
CPU instruction-set extensions that matter for scientific computing.
what's PCIe generation / lanes?
the bus specification linking CPU to GPU/NVMe. Gen 4 x16 is enthusiast desktop; Gen 3 x4 is entry.
what's NVMe form factor?
M.2 2280 / M.2 2242 / U.2 physical sizes.
what's SLC / MLC / TLC / QLC NAND?
flash memory density tiers. SLC (fastest, most durable) → QLC (largest, least durable).
what's write endurance / TBW?
total bytes written the drive is warrantied for.
what's matte / glossy display coating?
the surface finish.
what's color gamut / DCI-P3 / sRGB / Adobe RGB?
color-space coverage percentages (matters for photo/video work).
what's nits / cd/m²?
display brightness measure.
what's IPS / VA / TN panel?
LCD panel technologies with distinct viewing-angle and color-accuracy profiles.
what's OLED burn-in?
permanent image retention risk with OLED displays used with static content.
what's HackRF / SDR?
software-defined radio hardware; a hobbyist category.
what's eFuse / bootloader locked?
Android's secure-boot chain. Unlocking breaks warranty on most devices.
what's CoreBoot / UEFI / BIOS mod?
firmware modifications, enthusiast territory.
what's thermal pad / thermal paste?
the compound between chip and heatsink. Repaste is a common refurbish step.
what's capacitor plague?
historical fault where cheap electrolytic capacitors leak. Affects certain vintage hardware.
what's retrobright?
cleaning yellowed vintage plastic (mostly for retro-computing collectors).
what's ring?
a band worn around a finger. Sized 3-13 in the US.
what's necklace?
worn around the neck. Includes pendant necklaces and chain-only necklaces.
what's bracelet?
worn around the wrist. Includes cuffs (open), bangles (closed rigid), chains (flexible), and beaded.
what's earring?
worn on the ear. Includes studs, hoops, dangles, huggies.
what's pendant?
the object that hangs from a necklace chain.
what's chain?
the flexible link portion of a necklace or bracelet.
what's charm?
a small decorative object attached to a chain, bracelet, or larger piece.
what's gemstone?
a naturally occurring or lab-grown mineral cut for ornament.
what's silver?
the metal. Usually alloyed (see mid tier).
what's gold?
the metal. Karat-graded (see mid tier).
what's plated?
a thin surface coating of one metal over another base.
what's hallmarked?
carrying a small stamped mark indicating metal purity or maker.
what's handmade?
made by the seller's own hands, not manufactured.
what's custom?
made to the buyer's specification.
what's birthstone?
the gemstone traditionally associated with a birth month.
what's sterling silver?
92.5% silver, 7.5% copper (usually). Stamped "925."
what's fine silver?
99.9% silver. Softer, tarnishes less, stamps "999" or ".999."
what's argentium?
a modern sterling alloy with germanium; tarnishes less than standard sterling.
what's 14K / 18K / 22K gold?
what's rose gold?
gold alloyed with copper (for the pink tone).
what's white gold?
gold alloyed with nickel or palladium (for the silvery tone). Often rhodium-plated.
what's vermeil?
sterling silver base with a thick gold plating (min 10 microinches per US definition).
what's gold-filled?
a mechanically-bonded gold layer, thicker than plating. Marked "1/20 12K GF" etc.
what's rhodium plating?
a thin protective/whitening layer over white gold or silver.
what's cuff?
a bracelet with an open gap you slide over the wrist.
what's bangle?
a rigid closed bracelet you slide over the hand.
what's chain style?
cable, curb, figaro, snake, rope, box, singapore, wheat, herringbone. Each has a look and a price band.
what's clasp?
the closure mechanism. Lobster, spring ring, toggle, magnetic, hook-and-eye.
what's setting?
how a stone is held in place. Prong, bezel, channel, pavé, tension, halo.
what's cabochon?
a smooth-domed gemstone (no facets).
what's faceted?
a stone cut with flat surfaces for light return.
what's pavé?
small stones set close together like a paved surface.
what's halo?
a ring of smaller stones around a center stone.
what's carat?
the weight measure for gemstones (1 ct = 200 mg). Distinct from karat.
what's inclusion?
a natural mark inside a gemstone.
what's clarity grade?
how visible inclusions are. FL, IF, VVS1/2, VS1/2, SI1/2, I1/2/3.
what's cut grade?
quality of a diamond's cut. Excellent, Very Good, Good, Fair, Poor.
what's color grade?
the D-to-Z scale for diamonds (D = colorless).
what's lab-grown / lab-created?
chemically identical to natural, produced in a lab. Marked as such per FTC rule.
what's conflict-free / ethically sourced?
from suppliers who certify non-conflict provenance.
what's AAA / AA / A grade?
pearl quality grades (by luster and surface).
what's birthstone list?
Jan garnet, Feb amethyst, Mar aquamarine, Apr diamond, May emerald, Jun pearl, Jul ruby, Aug peridot, Sep sapphire, Oct opal, Nov citrine, Dec turquoise/tanzanite.
what's stackable?
designed to be worn with others in a stack (rings or bangles).
what's statement?
designed to draw attention as a solo piece.
what's minimalist?
small, spare, meant to layer with anything.
what's maximalist / bold?
large, decorated, meant to be the outfit's focal point.
what's layering necklace?
designed to layer with 1-3 others at varying lengths.
what's choker / princess / matinee / opera / rope?
necklace length standards (14 in / 18 in / 22 in / 30+ in / 36+ in).
what's repoussé?
reverse-side hammering to raise pattern on the face. Antonym of *chasing* which works from the front.
what's granulation?
tiny gold or silver balls fused to a surface (an ancient technique; Etruscan revival market).
what's filigree?
twisted wire soldered into openwork patterns.
what's anticlastic?
hammered with a stake that curves the sheet in two directions at once (produces a saddle-shape cuff).
what's synclastic?
the opposite curvature (bowl-shape).
what's forged?
shaped by hammering (vs. cast, which is poured molten into a mold).
what's hand-fabricated?
every element built by hand from sheet or wire (highest-value maker claim after unique-piece).
what's lost-wax cast?
cast from a wax model that's burned out of the mold.
what's shakudo / shibuichi?
Japanese copper alloys with intentional patinas (deep blue-black, silver-gray).
what's mokume-gane?
Japanese laminated-metal technique producing wood-grain patterns.
what's niello?
a black metallic inlay of copper-silver-lead-sulfur.
what's enamel / vitreous enamel / plique-à-jour?
glass fused to metal by kiln firing. *Plique-à-jour* is enamel with no backing, so light passes through (stained-glass effect).
what's cloisonné?
enamel bounded by soldered wire cells.
what's champlevé?
enamel filling engraved recesses in the metal.
what's gilding?
historic term for gold-plating; usually mercury-fire gilt or leaf.
what's hallmarking (specific stamps)?
UK: assay-office mark + fineness mark + date letter. US: no legal hallmark system; makers self-stamp. Continental Europe: fineness-only numeric stamp.
what's assay office?
the government body that tests and stamps metal purity (UK, Ireland, some EU).
what's die-struck?
stamped between two hardened steel dies (the mass-market end of "handmade").
what's milgrain?
a fine bead-textured edge (Edwardian/Art Deco signature).
what's filigree wire gauge?
usually 26-28 gauge for delicate work.
what's karat gold color families?
yellow, rose, white, green (nickel- free), Black Hills gold (a specific Dakota alloy of three golds).
what's matrix?
the surrounding rock visible in turquoise or opal. Spiderweb matrix is highly valued in turquoise.
what's doublet / triplet?
layered stones (e.g. opal doublet: opal slice on a black base for contrast).
what's treated stone?
heat-treated, irradiated, oiled, or diffusion- treated. Ethical listings disclose treatment.
what's Mohs hardness?
the 1-10 scratch scale. Diamond = 10, ruby/sapphire = 9, quartz = 7 (the "wearability threshold" for daily-wear stones).
what's specific gravity?
density measure used to identify unmounted gems.
what's refractive index?
light-bending measure used to identify cut gems (the second identification tool after specific gravity).
what's RI meter / refractometer?
the tool.
what's cerakote / hard anodizing?
modern surface treatments for titanium or aluminum jewelry.
what's niobium / titanium / stainless?
modern hypoallergenic metals (nickel-free).
what's surgical steel / 316L?
the medical-grade stainless commonly used for hypoallergenic body jewelry.
what's Cartier love / Van Cleef alhambra / Bulgari serpenti?
luxury design-language references. Makers name-check without infringing.
what's statement one-of-a-kind (OOAK)?
highest-value maker claim.
what's wearable art?
the honest-catch-all when the piece defies category.
what's Established D2C brand?
Everlane, Allbirds, Warby-adjacent clothing. Editorial vocabulary; heavy focus on materials ("recycled Italian cashmere"), fit ("relaxed drape"), and brand ethos.
what's Print-on-demand / drop-ship brand?
a store selling designs applied to blank apparel produced by an upstream manufacturer. Vocabulary is design-forward, less material- forward.
what's shirt / t-shirt / tee?
top garment.
what's sweater / knit / pullover / cardigan?
knitted top.
what's hoodie / sweatshirt?
heavy jersey top.
what's jacket / coat?
outer garment.
what's pants / trousers / jeans / chinos?
leg garment.
what's shorts?
short leg garment.
what's dress?
one-piece garment for the torso and legs.
what's skirt?
leg-covering garment from the waist.
what's shoes / sneakers / boots?
footwear.
what's accessories?
bags, belts, scarves, hats.
what's cotton / wool / linen / silk / polyester?
the fibers.
what's fit?
how the garment sits on the body (slim / relaxed / oversized).
what's size?
S / M / L / XL or numeric.
what's color?
the visible tint.
what's collection / line / drop?
a group of items released together.
what's sale / discount / promo?
reduced price.
what's new arrival / restock?
new-to-shop or back-in-stock.
what's shipping?
how the item gets to the buyer.
what's returns?
the seller's policy on giving money back.
what's drop?
a limited-time release, often sold out.
what's capsule collection / capsule?
a small tightly-curated line, often season-agnostic.
what's seasonal / SS26 / FW26?
spring/summer 2026 or fall/winter 2026 collection labels.
what's core / staples / everyday?
the always-in-stock basics a brand builds around.
what's restock?
an item back in inventory after selling out.
what's backorder / waitlist / preorder?
the ways to reserve before it ships.
what's regular fit / slim fit / relaxed / oversized / boxy?
cuts a brand offers.
what's inseam / rise / drop / hem?
pants measurements brands sometimes give per-size.
what's runs small / runs large / TTS?
fit-guidance codes ("true to size").
what's length: cropped / standard / long?
hem length codes.
what's weight: light / mid / heavyweight?
fabric weight tier (relates to season and drape).
what's hand feel / drape?
the tactile qualities of a fabric.
what's weave / knit type?
jersey, terry, French terry, waffle, ribbed, cable, boucle.
what's denim: raw / selvedge / stretch / rigid?
denim types.
what's wash / rinse / distressed / bleached?
denim finishes.
what's GSM / oz?
fabric-weight units (grams per square meter / ounces per square yard).
what's natural fiber / synthetic / blend?
the fiber-source categories.
what's organic cotton / GOTS certified?
cotton grown without synthetic pesticides, third-party certified.
what's Merino / mulesing-free?
Merino wool sourced with the ethical animal-welfare marker.
what's recycled polyester / rPET?
polyester made from recycled plastic bottles.
what's Tencel / lyocell / modal?
regenerated cellulose fibers from wood pulp; environmentally softer than viscose/rayon.
what's Sherpa / faux fur / bouclé / boiled wool?
texture categories.
what's twill / poplin / oxford / seersucker?
woven fabric patterns.
what's elasticated / cinched / drawstring?
closure and gathering styles.
what's taped seams / French seams / flat-felled?
construction quality indicators.
what's serged / overlocked?
seam-finishing techniques (basic quality baseline).
what's country of origin: Made in Portugal / Made in Japan?
country claims that often carry quality signals in the D2C space.
what's fair-trade / union-made / small-batch?
labor claims.
what's carbon-neutral shipping / plastic-free packaging?
sustainability claims about non-product aspects.
what's size chart / fit finder / measurement guide?
tools the shop provides to help sizing.
what's staple length?
the length of individual cotton fibers. Longer staple (Pima, Supima, Egyptian) = softer, stronger.
what's combed cotton?
cotton where short fibers were combed out. Smoother yarn.
what's carded cotton?
cotton where all fibers are used. Rougher, less expensive.
what's ring-spun?
yarn spun using a ring frame; softer than open-end.
what's open-end / rotor-spun?
cheaper, coarser yarn method.
what's combed ring-spun?
combed + ring-spun; the higher-quality baseline for premium tees.
what's Pima / Supima / Egyptian / Sea Island?
long-staple cotton grades.
what's worsted / woolen?
spinning methods for wool yarn. Worsted is smooth, woolen is fuzzy.
what's super numbers (Super 100s / 120s / 150s / 180s)?
wool fineness grade in worsted wool (higher = finer + softer + more delicate).
what's cashmere: A / B / C grade?
cashmere fiber grades. Grade A: 14-15 microns diameter; the softest.
what's denier / dtex?
measures of yarn linear density (matters for synthetics; tights are given in denier).
what's thread count?
threads per square inch in woven fabric (mostly a bedding term but overlaps with dress-shirt world).
what's twill weave 2/1 / 3/1?
the twill construction ratio (matters for chambray, denim, gabardine).
what's selvedge denim?
denim woven on shuttle looms with self-finished edges; premium marker.
what's Kojima / Okayama / Cone Mills / candiani?
premium denim mills.
what's enzyme wash / stone wash / acid wash / dip dye?
garment-treatment finishes.
what's crockmeter / colorfastness rating?
how well a fabric holds dye under friction.
what's pilling resistance / tumble test?
how well a knit resists surface fuzz balls.
what's hand-linked seams?
high-end sweater construction where knitters connect panels stitch-by-stitch.
what's fully-fashioned?
knitwear shaped in the knit rather than cut-and-sew.
what's cut-and-sew?
knitwear made by cutting shapes from a larger knit and sewing them; cheaper method.
what's stitching density?
stitches per inch; higher = stronger seam.
what's RN / CA number?
mandatory manufacturer identifiers on US labels.
what's YKK / Riri / Lampo?
zipper brands. YKK is baseline; Riri and Lampo are premium.
what's grosgrain / bemberg / cupro?
internal-finish fabrics used in linings; enthusiast markers.
what's canvas construction (jacket)?
full canvas / half canvas / fused. Canvas is the internal structural layer; full canvas is premium.
what's pattern making / fit sample / lookbook / tech pack?
brand-side vocabulary that occasionally surfaces in founder content.
